title: Life expectancy gap for Black Bostonians is growing, health officials warn
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Black Bostonians' life expectancy gap widened from 3.3 years in 2013 to 6.6 years by 2024.
Leading causes of premature mortality for Black residents include unintentional overdose, cancer, and cardiometabolic disease.
Boston Public Health Commission announced $1 million investments to address health disparities affecting Black communities.

title: Former Celtics center Luke Kornet calls out Hawks over ‘Magic City’ promotion
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Former Celtics center Luke Kornet criticized the Atlanta Hawks' 'Magic City Night' promotional event scheduled for March 16.
Kornet objected to the promotion as it celebrates Magic City, a notable strip club in Atlanta, without acknowledging its nature.
Kornet urged the NBA and the Hawks to reconsider the event, citing concerns over objectification and mistreatment of women.

title: Married couple from R.I. identified as victims in fatal Swansea crash
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Carolyn Carcasi, 54, and James Carcasi, 53, from Bristol, R.I., were victims in a fatal Swansea car crash.
The crash happened on Feb. 27 at Route 136 and Route 6. Both vehicles sustained catastrophic damage.
Driver Demitri Sousa, 28, allegedly involved in both the crash and a separate Rhode Island shooting, is hospitalized.

title: Steve Kerr apologizes for comments about Kristaps Porzingis’ lingering illness
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Steve Kerr apologized for making uninformed comments about Kristaps Porzingis' health condition during a radio interview.
Kristaps Porzingis revealed he suffers from postural orthostatic tachycardia syndrome, affecting his performance and limiting him to 18 games.
Porzingis was traded to the Golden State Warriors and has played only one game, scoring 12 points against the Celtics.

title: Taking stock of Bruins’ top trade chips entering the NHL deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins consider utilizing draft picks and prospects, like Mason Lohrei, as trade assets before March 6 NHL deadline.
Bruins GM Don Sweeney is open to both acquiring new talent and trading assets, including valuable Toronto 2026 first-round pick.
Potential trade candidates include Mason Lohrei, Andrew Peeke, Viktor Arvidsson, Casey Mittelstadt, and Pavel Zacha for various strategic reasons.

title: NFL insider: Maxx Crosby trade could happen ‘this week’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Maxx Crosby, 29-year-old Raiders pass-rusher, is a potential trade target for the New England Patriots this week.
Multiple NFL teams, including Dallas and Chicago, are interested in acquiring Crosby from the Raiders.
The Raiders, coming off a 3-14 season, hold the No. 1 draft pick and receive many calls about Crosby.

title: Quincy man sentenced in fatal St. Patrick’s Day assault in downtown Boston
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Sanusi Sadiq, 31, sentenced to 3-6 years for manslaughter in 2023 St. Patrick's Day assault in Boston.
Victim Barry Whelan, 46, died from a traumatic brain injury after being struck and falling on Winter Street.
Sadiq was found guilty in January 2026 retrial; previous trial ended in mistrial in February 2025.

title: Bill Simmons seems convinced that ‘all signs are pointing to’ this date for Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Bill Simmons suggests Jayson Tatum could return to play Friday against the Mavericks at TD Garden.
The Celtics have not updated Tatum's status, who remains out Tuesday against Milwaukee due to achilles tendon repair.
The game against Dallas will be nationally televised on ESPN at 7 p.m.; it's a rematch of the 2024 NBA Finals.

title: Should teens be allowed to carry handguns in Massachusetts?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court to review legality of handgun permits for individuals aged 18-20.
The case originated from Mikai Thomson's conviction for possessing a handgun at age 20.
Gun-rights advocates cite Second Amendment, referencing the 2022 Supreme Court ruling on firearm rights.

title: 5 takeaways from Don Sweeney’s Bruins press conference before trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins GM Don Sweeney is exploring trade options before the NHL trade deadline but remains focused on long-term goals.
Sweeney is hesitant to trade for rental players and prefers acquiring talent under long-term contracts for the Bruins.
The Bruins aim to reinforce their lineup without sacrificing future assets, balancing current competitiveness and long-term prospects.
